summary: A single-page website featuring a fire CSS/JS animation with a button that displays a random fact when clicked.

existing_features.title: Create a visually striking fire CSS/JS animation as the centerpiece of the page. The fire effect should use layered animated elements (flames, embers, glow) built with CSS keyframe animations and JavaScript-driven particle effects to simulate realistic, flickering fire.

Add a 'Get a Random Fact' button styled to match the fire theme (e.g., glowing ember or molten look). When clicked, it displays a new random fact drawn from a built-in collection of at least 20 interesting facts. The fact text should appear with a smooth reveal animation.

Apply a cohesive fire-themed visual design across the entire page: dark background (near-black or deep charcoal), warm orange/red/amber color palette for text and UI elements, dramatic typography, and a layout that frames the fire animation and fact display card as focal points. Enforce the theme consistently across all components.

new_feature_requests.summary: Store the collection of random facts in the Motoko backend as a stable array. Expose a query method `getRandomFact()` that returns a random fact string. The frontend calls this method when the button is clicked.
